title: In heatwave, some French museums and monuments become cool havens while others are forced to close
subhead: Several cultural venues have transformed into climate refuges by offering free admission, while others have had to stop welcoming visitors due to the extreme heat.

France detects first Ebola case in doctor returning from DRC
subhead: This is the first case ever diagnosed on French soil. Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu is monitoring the situation 'very closely,' according to his office, as quoted by Agence France-Presse.

title: The unavoidable rise of a less American world
subhead: The war in the Middle East has sped up a realignment that was already underway, forcing the US's partners to rethink flexible alliances without creating overly strong dependencies.

title: Resistance fighter Marc Bloch inducted into Panthéon in moving moment of republican solidarity
subhead: On Tuesday, the tribute ceremony recounted the life of the historian, who was executed by the Germans on June 16, 1944. In a speech that served as an indictment against Vichy France and antisemitism, President Emmanuel Macron lamented the 'spirit of defeat' that, he said, permeates 'our public life.'

title: UN commission denounces ongoing 'genocide' in Gaza: 'By targeting children, Israel is attacking the very capacity of the Palestinian people to exist'
subhead: In a report published Tuesday, an international commission of inquiry, mandated by the United Nations Human Rights Council, identified Israeli security force units that it accused of being responsible for the deaths of Palestinian children. Israel condemned the report as 'defamatory.'

Living under Paris's zinc roofs in 40°C heat: 'One day, the people living there will look for those responsible'
subhead: As France faces extreme heat, many of these top-story spaces have become uninhabitable. Some residents have formed groups to call on their landlords, neighbors and local authorities to address their living conditions.

title: Ten years after Brexit, the City of London financial district is stronger than ever
subhead: When the UK left the EU following the referendum on June 23, 2016, the City of London financial district had to relinquish some of its activities that were focused on the European market. But it has since bounced back, recording strong growth in insurance and fintech.

title: Montréal gunman leaves behind manifesto inspired by the 'incel' movement
subhead: A shooting in the Canadian city on Monday, June 22, left three dead, including a police officer and the attacker. In a manifesto, the shooter expressed his hatred of women.
